title: Monitor each feature for drift over time
description: Compare live feature distributions to the training reference and route material drift to investigation or retraining.
body: Use when features feed a long-lived production model whose inputs can shift. Do not assume features are stationary — upstream changes silently shift distributions and degrade accuracy. Actions: store the training reference distribution per feature, compute drift (PSI/KS) on live values on a schedule, and alert when a feature crosses its threshold; reconcile with upstream change logs. Validation signals include per-feature drift scores against the training reference and an alert that fired on a seeded shift. Risk boundary: an undetected drifting feature poisons predictions; if drift co-occurs with an upstream schema change, escalate to confirm the feature is still valid.
